mapping.a_element: covariance matrix Σ of the subset-indicator vector and its Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse Σ^†, treated as a Laplacian-type operator
mapping.b_element: weighted (hyper)graph Laplacian built from the fermion-to-qubit encoding

mapping.a_element: effective resistance (e_i-e_j)^T Σ^† (e_i-e_j) — a Laplacian spectral quantity
mapping.b_element: algebraic connectivity (Fiedler-type Laplacian eigenvalue) of the kinetic vs. interaction hypergraphs

mapping.a_element: constant-stretch correlated sampling — a coupling between probability measures on the hypersimplex
mapping.b_element: optimal transport of probability measures in XBK coupling space

brief_justification: The only overlaps are generic shared toolkits — Laplacian spectral quantities (effective resistance vs. algebraic connectivity) and couplings between measures (correlated sampling vs. optimal transport) — not a common governing process: paper A proves a probabilistic covariance inequality (e_i-e_j)^T Σ^† (e_i-e_j) ≤ 1/v_i+1/v_j to guarantee correlated sampling from an external-field measure, whereas paper B constructs a hypergraph-geometry framework to characterize the physical/structural evolution of fermion-to-qubit encodings, so no role-for-role mapping drives both.
